title: helianthemum apenninum var. croceum
intro: Helianthemum apenninum var. croceum, also known as the Apennine rockrose, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Cistaceae. It is a low-growing, shrubby plant with yellow flowers, traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Helianthemum apenninum var. croceum include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, with the flowers and leaves being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
